name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: align_to_usize rounds down, so the result must never exceed the input

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The whole purpose of align_to_usize is to produce an aligned address; if the result is not aligned the function is broken

name: aligned_successor_also_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_successor_also_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr + 1, size),
{
}
reason: If addr is aligned to size>1 then addr+1 cannot also be aligned, so this being provable would mean aligned is trivially true

name: align_drops_too_much
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_drops_too_much(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
{
}
reason: The rounding loss must be strictly less than b; losing b or more means we skipped an entire alignment boundary

name: inflight_walks_preconditions_vacuous
code:
proof fn phi_5_inflight_walks_preconditions_vacuous(pre: State, post: State, c: Constants, step: Step, lbl: Lbl)
    requires
        pre.wf(c),
        pre.happy,
        post.happy,
        post.polarity is Mapping,
        pre.inv_sbuf_facts(c),
        post.inv_sbuf_facts(c),
        pre.inv_mapping__valid_is_not_in_sbuf(c),
        pre.inv_mapping__inflight_walks(c),
        next_step(pre, post, c, step, lbl),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If the combined preconditions are unsatisfiable the invariant preservation theorem is vacuously true and proves nothing
